rulings.Case Title: CHERYL WALLACE et al VS. VICTOR WU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 664.6 Entered Into Between Plaintiffs John Owen And Cheryl Wallace, And For Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 25, 2020 line 1. PLAINTIFF JOHN OWEN Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 664.6 Entered Into Between Plaintiffs John Owen And Cheryl Wallace, And For Attorneys Fees And Costs is OFF CALENDAR. Counsel of record for Owen and Wallace is David L. Rouda. This motion was filed by a stranger to the case, John Cowden and Wallace was served (according to the proof of service) at an address that is not her address of record. Finally, this case was dismissed in its entirety on March 2, 2016 and the Court never retained jurisdiction to enforce the settlement agreement under CCP 664.6. See Sayta v. Chu (2017) 17 Cal.App.5th 960. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ANGELA LEUNG VS. STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 25, 2020 line 2. DEFENDANT STONESTOWN MEDICAL BUILDING, LLC. DEMURRER to 5TH Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. No substantive opposition filed. See ruling on co-defendant's demurrer for details. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ANGELA LEUNG VS. STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 25, 2020 line 2. DEFENDANT STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, LP DEMURRER to 5TH Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with final leave to amend her complaint being mindful of the sham pleading and judicial estoppel doctrines. Plaintiff's oppositions to the demurer to the Fourth Amended Complaint and to the Fifth Amended Complaint highlight the uncertainty of the allegations: is there just one contract with multiple terms, is there a wirtten contract and an oral "side" agreement; did failure to allow early access cause damages complained of; was early access promise an integral part of the new tenancy for unit 425, etc. Plaintiff shall also identify the business practice complained of (in light of authorities cited in reply) and the damages caused by this business practice (also in light of authorities cited in reply). There will be no further amendments allowed. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: TAL N. TAMIR ET AL VS. LEM HOLDINGS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Contractual Arbitration And To Stay Action, In Lieu Of Answer
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 25, 2020 line 5. DEFENDANT LEM HOLDINGS, LLC, CIARAN HARTY, PATRICK HARTY Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Contractual Arbitration And To Stay Action, In Lieu Of Answer is continued to October 9, 2020. Moving party to provide a complete set of courtesy copies of all filings in support of and in opposition to the motion no later than September 25, 2020 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. =(501/CFH)
